title: Infosys McCamish Systems Enters Consent Order With South Dakota Division of Insurance
detailed_summary: Infosys disclosed that Infosys McCamish Systems, a subsidiary of Infosys BPM limited, entered into a Consent Order with the State of South Dakota Division of Insurance on December 22, 2025. The matter relates to concerns that IMS may have violated South Dakota Codified Laws regarding notification connected to a 2023 data breach and failed to respond to the Division of Insurance's request for information within 20 days. IMS resolved the matter without admission of the alleged violations and submitted a monetary penalty of $30,000.

key_facts.fact: On December 22, 2025, Infosys McCamish Systems entered into a Consent Order with the State of South Dakota Division of Insurance.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub: Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: Infosys McCamish Systems is a subsidiary of Infosys BPM limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of Infosys limited.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub: Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: The South Dakota Division of Insurance expressed concerns that IMS may have violated South Dakota Codified Laws related to notification with respect to a 2023 data breach.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub: Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: The Consent Order specifically acknowledges that this is not an admission that IMS violated the Acts as listed above.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub: Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: IMS submitted a payment of $30,000 to the South Dakota Division of Insurance as agreed upon in the consent order as a monetary penalty.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub: Update on McCamish Cyber Incident
